steps.title: Focus on the pre-Harlem period
steps.content: The student's goal is narrower than summarizing Ellison's whole career. The sentence must emphasize what he had accomplished before he moved to Harlem in 1914. The relevant notes identify two earlier accomplishments: Songs of Jamaica and Constab Ballads, both acclaimed poetry collections published in 1912 while Ellison was living in Jamaica.

steps.title: Reject mixed or later details
steps.content: A lists early and later works together, so the pre-Harlem accomplishments do not stand out. C focuses on the move to Harlem and on Ellison's later Harlem Renaissance reputation. D begins with the phrase before moving to Harlem, but the works it names, Harlem Shadows and Home to Harlem, are later works from 1922 and 1928.

steps.title: Use the sentence with contrast
steps.content: The needed sentence should keep the 1912 Jamaica publications separate from Ellison's later fame. B does exactly that: it contrasts his Harlem Renaissance reputation with the fact that he had already published two acclaimed poetry collections while living in Jamaica. This directly emphasizes the accomplishments before Harlem instead of merely naming places or later works. The answer is B.
